Home › Orange County › Orlando › BENTO CAFE
A 91

BENTO CAFE

This grade is calculated by us from Florida DBPR public inspection records — Florida does not issue official grades. How we calculate it →

Data last updated: May 7, 2026

BENTO CAFE is a quiet keeper — an A kitchen, 91/100, no fuss, last checked May 7, 2026.

On health score it's safer than 46% of Orange County restaurants we grade. Diners rate it 4.3 from 2,072 reviews. Cuisine: Chinese. Menus here flag vegetarian, dairy-free, keto, paleo, and other dietary options.

Get directions Call (407) 352-2277

Health grade
A (91/100)

7 total — 1 intermediate, 6 basic
Address
7335 W SAND LAKE RD, Orlando, FL
Cuisine
Chinese

Community Rating

Reviews via Google
4.3 ★★★★☆ Community-sourced rating 2,072 reviews
Read all reviews on Google →
  • ★★★★★ June 28, 2026

    Get take out here...always good!

  • ★★★★★ June 27, 2026

    Man oh man! I had some food here and the service was great. The food was even better. Boy, I’d come here again oh yeah.

  • ★★★★★ June 26, 2026

    Andrea is absolutely phenomenal! She’s always gone above and beyond for me. Service and sushi top tier ! Thank you for making my day extra lovely!! Mahalo!!!

Inspection history

The following records are from Florida DBPR public inspection records, most recent first.

    Inspection Completed - No Further Action

    0 high-priority · 1 intermediate · 6 basic

    • 14 — Food-contact & nonfood-contact surfaces properly designed/maintained
    • 16 — Dishwashing facilities; sanitizer test kits; gauges
    • 25 — Single-service and single-use items
    • 32 — Bathrooms
    • 36 — Floors, walls, ceilings clean & maintained; proper ventilation (×2)
    • 42 — Cleaning and maintenance equipment

  2. October 24, 2025 · Routine - Food

    Inspection Completed - No Further Action

    2 high-priority · 2 intermediate · 9 basic

    • 03 — Temperature control of foods (cold/hot holding, cooking, cooling, reheating)
    • 05 — Food & equipment thermometers provided and accurate
    • 10 — In-use food dispensing utensils properly stored
    • 14 — Food-contact & nonfood-contact surfaces properly designed/maintained (×2)
    • 21 — Wiping cloths; clean & soiled linens; laundry
    • 22 — Food-contact surfaces clean and sanitized
    • 24 — Storage & handling of clean equipment and utensils; air drying
    • 25 — Single-service and single-use items
    • 31 — Handwash sinks accessible; handwashing supplies & sign provided
    • 32 — Bathrooms
    • 35 — Insects/rodents/pests; outer openings protected against pests
    • 36 — Floors, walls, ceilings clean & maintained; proper ventilation
